title: Shared Memory, Mutexes, Atomics, the Memory Model, and Race Evidence · Spiral Systems Review
summary: Concurrent workers update shared counters, caches, and snapshots; plausible output hides races and broken publication. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a race-free shared-state component after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Identify conflicting memory operations and establish sequenced-before, synchronized-before, and happens-before evidence.
Protect one documented invariant with Mutex or RWMutex while minimizing lock scope and avoiding copy or reentrancy errors.
Use Once, OnceValue, Cond, and channel alternatives only when their wakeup, predicate-loop, and failure semantics fit.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Data Races Happens Before · changed case · Review Data Races Happens Before
steps.instruction:
review case — Workers concurrently publish a map-backed snapshot and increment two related counters. Output looks plausible in 6 runs. Establish happens-before edges, choose channel, mutex, or atomic ownership, and state what go test -race can and cannot prove.
